page: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v4 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the EDSv4 server family. It is equipped with 20 dedicated vCPUs, mapped from 10 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL processor cores operating at 2.6 GHz. The configuration features hyper-threading with two threads per core, supported by a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 35 MB L3 cache.

This instance is memory-optimized, providing 160.0 GB of RAM, which equates to an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. It includes 768 GB of local SSD storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk attachments. No GPU accelerators are present on this server.

Benchmark results place the E20ds_v4 in the average performance tier across most workloads. It achieves a single-core Geekbench score of 1271.0 and a multi-core score of 8298.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 19266.4. Memory bandwidth tests show 1038.89 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 80.0 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations perform steadily, with Redis SET operations reaching 17.23 million operations per second and static web serving throughput measured at 14.33 GB/sec. However, LLM text generation and prompt processing for medium models show weaker relative performance, indicating limitations for complex AI inference tasks.

Given its high memory-to-CPU ratio and bundled local storage, the E20ds_v4 offers solid qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for relational database servers, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring a balance of high memory capacity and local temporary storage.
description: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 160.0 GB of RAM and 768 GB of local SSD storage. The server's hardware architecture delivers a balanced 8.0 GB of memory per core. Benchmark evaluations indicate average performance across standard computing tasks, with a Passmark CPU score of 19266.4 and a Redis SET throughput of 17.23 million operations per second. While its memory bandwidth and database processing capabilities are robust, its performance in LLM inference tasks is relatively weak. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, and caching workloads that benefit from local temporary storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160 GB of RAM, and 768 GB of local SSD storage, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E20ds_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 20 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and 768 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory density and local SSD storage for demanding database and caching workloads.

160.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
768 GB of local SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Average performance in database operations and Redis caching
Optimized for memory-intensive enterprise applications and databases
